scenarios.task: Our trial's per-protocol analysis shows a more favorable treatment effect than the intention-to-treat analysis, since patients who dropped out on the treatment arm tended to be doing worse. I'm reporting the per-protocol result as the headline finding since it shows a stronger effect.
criteria: Flags reporting per-protocol as the headline when it diverges favorably from ITT as the failure mode
States that ITT is the primary analysis population by default, and per-protocol looking better can simply reflect the differential-dropout confound ITT exists to prevent
Recommends reporting ITT as the primary result with per-protocol labeled as a sensitivity analysis
Notes this is exactly the pattern ITT is designed to guard against

scenarios.task: Kaplan-Meier curves for two treatment arms visibly cross partway through follow-up (the effect is strong early and fades later), but I'm reporting a single overall hazard ratio as the summary result without checking the proportional-hazards assumption.
criteria: Flags reporting a single hazard ratio without checking the proportional-hazards assumption as the failure mode
States that when curves cross or diverge over time, a single averaged hazard ratio hides a time-varying effect behind a misleading average number
Recommends checking the proportional-hazards assumption (Schoenfeld residuals) and using restricted mean survival time or a time-varying-coefficient model if it fails
Notes the crossing curves themselves are a visual sign the assumption likely fails

scenarios.task: After a previous trial ran into a multiplicity problem, I'm now applying uniform Bonferroni correction across every secondary and exploratory endpoint in this new trial, regardless of the number of endpoints or their correlation.
criteria: Flags applying uniform Bonferroni correction across all secondary/exploratory endpoints as overcorrection
States that this is so conservative it can obscure a genuinely important secondary finding worth a confirmatory follow-up study
Recommends a gatekeeping/hierarchical procedure or a less conservative correction appropriate to the actual number and correlation of endpoints
Notes the previous trial's multiplicity problem doesn't justify blanket over-correction on every future trial

scenarios.task: The sponsor's efficacy analysis team asked for informal visibility into the unblinded interim safety data 'just to stay informed' during the trial, and I'm planning to share a summary with them since they're not making any decisions based on it, just staying aware.
criteria: Flags sharing unblinded interim safety data with the sponsor's efficacy team as compromising DSMB independence

States that DSMB independence is the entire point of the structure, and informal visibility for the sponsor's efficacy team creates a route for endpoint or population switching before final unblinding
Recommends declining to share this data with the efficacy team regardless of the stated 'just staying informed' intent
Notes that even non-decision-making informal visibility undermines the independence the structure depends on
